title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Runtime Lens: Reflow Zoom
summary: Generated pages assume one viewport, content clips at zoom, navigation depends on JavaScript, fonts block text, images shift layout, and a fast home page hides slow long-form routes. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reflow zoom increment in responsive progressively enhanced performance budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-reflow-zoom — Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-responsive-performance-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_responsive_performance_2(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-reflow-zoom-xh8oig
# Competency: ssg-reflow-zoom.
# Case 2028: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_reflow_zoom_xh8oig(viewports: int = 3, reflow_passes: int = 3, page_kib: int = 180, page_budget_kib: int = 220, layout_shifts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xh8oig = "xh8oig-gio8hx-2028"
    assert evidence_variant_xh8oig
    evidence_axes_xh8oig = (
        "readerxh8oig",
        "source2028",
        "parsegio8hx",
        "routexh8oig2028",
        "artifact2028xh8oig",
        "recoverygio8hx2028",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xh8oig)) == 6
    if viewports < 3 or reflow_passes < 0:
        return False, reflow_passes, "viewport-matrix"
    if reflow_passes != viewports:
        return False, reflow_passes, "reflow-failure"
    if page_kib < 0 or page_kib > page_budget_kib:
        return False, page_kib, "resource-budget"
    if layout_shifts > 0:
        return False, layout_shifts, "layout-instability"
    assert reflow_passes == viewports
    return True, page_kib, "responsive-budget"

stimulus.title: Reflow Zoom case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory publishing team handles case 1313 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A page that fits at 390 pixels will also work at zoom and with long content.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling. Artifact under review: responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Reflow Zoom, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Reflow Zoom record says responsive progressively enhanced performance budget looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect reflow zoom evidence boundary for Reflow Zoom.

For inspect reflow zoom evidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Reflow Zoom for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
